title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss Apple's AI strategy at WWDC, Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement, and the intensifying competition between Microsoft and OpenAI. The conversation examines whether tech companies can balance safety concerns with market pressures in AI development.
writeup: This podcast analyzes Apple's AI strategy from WWDC, focusing on OS-level AI integration, alongside Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement capabilities and the growing tension between Microsoft and OpenAI over model development versus cloud services. The conversation matters because it tracks how the major AI players are positioning themselves across consumer devices, foundation models, and infrastructure. Key takeaways: Apple is pushing AI deeper into the operating system, Anthropic is raising concerns tied to self-improving AI, and the Microsoft-OpenAI relationship is straining over who controls model development. The discussion also addresses ethical concerns as AI companies move toward IPO.
